strategy: Stop worker intake, cancel or drain live process groups, reconcile durable queued and running attempts under the current scheduler fence, quarantine any contaminated worktree, then deploy the exact pre-Sprint-04 commit. No Sprint 04 schema migration was added, so database downgrade is not required; forward repair remains preferred after worker lifecycle evidence becomes operationally authoritative.

tests.name: isolated code rollback target
tests.command: git worktree add --detach <temporary-directory> b419628985b8da11fd5e237e63563db7499f399b && make -C <temporary-directory> validate
tests.summary: The exact pre-Sprint-04 public commit passed its complete validation gate in an isolated Git worktree.

tests.name: process termination before rollback
tests.summary: Cancellation, timeout, and residual-child tests prove bounded process-group termination before runtime replacement.

tests.name: durable attempt reconciliation
tests.summary: Abandoned queued and running attempts become retryable only under a replacement live scheduler fence, without trusting stale operating-system process identifiers.

tests.name: contaminated worktree preservation
tests.summary: A failed dirty attempt cannot reuse its worktree; bytes remain available for quarantine evidence instead of destructive cleanup.

runtime_state_impact: The rollback target does not execute Sprint 04 workers or lifecycle transitions. Operators must first reconcile or drain active attempts and retain append-only audit evidence; automated worktree replacement remains unavailable until Sprint 05.
